text.title: Agile and Hybrid Approaches Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules grounding the Agile and Hybrid Approaches methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Prefer Hybrid only when at least two factors score Predictive AND two score Agile; otherwise default to the majority signal.
rule.rationale: humans and LLMs both default to Hybrid because it sounds safe; the 2+2 threshold prevents false hybrids.

rule.statement: Pick one approach per project phase, not per ceremony; translate between approaches at explicit, named boundaries.
rule.rationale: mixed-mode within a phase produces competing artefacts (burn-down + Gantt) and stakeholder confusion.

rule.statement: Hybrid stakeholder reporting MUST use burn-up charts, not burn-down; burn-down hides scope additions.
rule.rationale: burn-down hides scope growth; burn-up makes scope explicit at every step.

rule.statement: Define 'Done' once per phase; sharing a single DoD across agile and predictive phases is forbidden.
rule.rationale: agile 'done' (tested, deployed to staging) differs materially from waterfall 'done' (signed off, documented); conflation causes undetected gaps.

rule.statement: Each of the 5 factors MUST cite evidence (charter clause, transcript line, ticket comment); agent-supplied scores without evidence are flagged 'verify-before-commit'.
rule.rationale: agent-assigned factor scores are persuasive but unfalsifiable without anchored evidence.

rule.statement: The selected approach MUST be persisted as an ADR with the 5 factor scores, the chosen approach, and named decision owner; in-passing decisions are forbidden.
rule.rationale: without an ADR the rationale is lost within 3 months and the team re-litigates the choice.
